en.extract:
Baumann's olive greenbul (Phyllastrephus baumanni) is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in West Africa from Guinea and Sierra Leone to south-eastern Nigeria. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and moist savanna. Although little known, new research has shown it to be plentiful and widespread. Consequently, it is listed as a species of Least Concern by the IUCN. Alternate names for Baumann's olive greenbul include Baumann's bulbul, Baumann's greenbul and olive greenbul.


Description
It has an olive-brown head and a gray face. Its eyes are brown. Its underparts are gray and its back is brown.

'''Baumann's olive greenbul''' ('''''Phyllastrephus baumanni''''') is a [[species]] of [[songbird]] in the [[bulbul]] [[Family (taxonomy)|family]], [[Pycnonotidae]]. It is found in West Africa from Guinea and Sierra Leone to south-eastern Nigeria. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and moist [[savanna]]. Although little known, new research has shown it to be plentiful and widespread. Consequently, it is listed as a species of [[Least Concern]] by the [[IUCN]].<ref name="iucn status" /> Alternate names for Baumann's olive greenbul include '''Baumann's bulbul''', '''Baumann's greenbul''' and '''olive greenbul'''.

== Description ==
It has an olive-brown head and a gray face.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Fishpool |first=Lincoln D. C. |date=September 1999 |title=Little-known Bird: Baumann's Greenbul Phyllastrephus baumanni |journal=Bulletin of the African Bird Club |volume=6 |issue=2 |pages=137–137 |doi=10.5962/p.309585 |issn=1352-481X|doi-access=free }}</ref> Its eyes are brown.<ref name=":0" /> Its underparts are gray and its back is brown.<ref name=":0" />
